name: Ardoq API
what_it_does: Collaborative enterprise architecture and tech portfolio platform with REST API for managing components, workspaces, references, and generating architecture insights for digital transformation.
use_cases: Querying and updating IT component data as part of automated architecture audits
Syncing cloud infrastructure inventory into Ardoq architecture models
Generating architecture reports and dependency maps via API for exec dashboards
Automating CMDB-like component lifecycle management for IT portfolio governance
Tracking technology radar and retirement schedules programmatically
not_for: Developer-centric service catalogs (use Backstage or Port instead)
best_when: An agent maintains and evolves enterprise IT landscape models for large organizations using Ardoq as their architecture repository.
avoid_when: You're a startup needing a lightweight service catalog rather than a full enterprise architecture platform.

auth.notes: API tokens generated per organization. Tokens control access to specific workspaces. Ardoq uses organization-level API keys with optional workspace scoping.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; trial by request
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing: $25k-200k+/year depending on organization size
pricing.notes: Ardoq competes with LeanIX and Alfabet in the enterprise architecture market. Pricing is negotiated annually. Targets Fortune 500 and large enterprises.

performance.rate_limits: Standard enterprise rate limits; documented in API documentation

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is available for customers. Core operations (create, read, update components and references) are well-documented. Error responses are JSON.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Component creation with external IDs supports idempotent upserts. Workspace structure must be pre-configured.
known_agent_gotchas: Workspace structure (component types, reference types) must be configured in UI first
Ardoq data model (components, fields, references) requires domain familiarity
Large architecture imports should use batch endpoints to avoid timeouts
API documentation is not publicly available — requires customer account access

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 and ISO27001 certified. EU and US data residency options. GDPR compliant. Enterprise-grade security practices. Token-based API authentication.
